summary: Iteration 59: Auth System Upgrade Testing - 20/20 tests PASSED (100%). All production-grade auth features verified: login with 'identifier' field (email or username), access_token + refresh_token flow, token refresh endpoint, logout with token invalidation, protected routes, and rate limiting (5/minute).
critical_code_review_comments: Auth system correctly uses 'identifier' field instead of 'email' for login
Access token (15min) + Refresh token (7 days) implemented correctly
Refresh tokens stored in DB for invalidation on logout
Token type validation prevents using access_token for refresh
Rate limiting (5/minute) active on login endpoint
Case-insensitive login works for both email and username
seed_data_creation: No test data created - used existing seeded users
context_for_next_testing_agent: Auth system upgraded to production-grade: (1) Login uses 'identifier' field (supports email or username), (2) Returns access_token (15min) + refresh_token (7 days), (3) POST /api/auth/refresh returns new access_token, (4) POST /api/auth/logout invalidates refresh token in DB, (5) Old 'email' field in login payload returns 422, (6) Rate limit 5/minute on login endpoint.

tested_endpoints.GET /api/health/db-status: 200 - Returns user count and implant library info
tested_endpoints.GET /api/auth/me: 200 - Returns user profile with valid access token
tested_endpoints.POST /api/auth/refresh: 200 - Returns new access_token with valid refresh_token

response_structure.refresh_token: JWT with 7 day expiry, type='refresh', stored in DB
response_structure.user: UserResponse object with id, name, email, role, profile_photo
